dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 5 for Assault/Rape call at Exon near 10th and Broad.
Hi, I'm Jason, a paramedic with Metro EMS. What happened here today?
We were trying to help her - she says she has pain all over her body.
Ma'am, can you open your eyes for me? What is your name? What time is it now?
Huh… My name is Briana… I don't know the time.
Okay, Briana. Where are we right now?
I'm in Richmond... I think.
We need to check your airway. Can you tell us if you're having a hard time breathing at all?
Respirations are even and unlabored. Chest movement is bilateral and equal.
Pt denies breathing issues. Airway is clear. Let's take blood pressure.
Blood pressure is 108 over 66. Looks stable for now.
Glucose cannot be obtained at this time.
Did you lose consciousness at any point tonight, Briana?
Yes. I don't know how long I was out. When I came back, my face was hurting.
Can you follow my finger with your eyes only? Left to right.
I see some blood on her lip and hands - marked as a finding.
We'll hold off on cleaning, but let's note it for the ED.
Do you remember when this pain started, Briana? About how long ago?
Like 30 minutes ago, but I don't know when exactly. I don't even know what happened.
Have you had anything to drink today?
I think so, but I don't know how much. I was upset. My body just hurts everywhere.
On a scale from 0 to 10, with 10 being the worst pain you ever had, how bad is this pain?
It's a 10. It's not just my body - my bones, everything feels broken.
Respirations are 22 and equal.
We're going to do a quick EKG to check your heart now. Yours is tachycardic - that's faster than normal, but we're watching it.
Performing initial 4-lead EKG monitoring.
Let's officially transition to Protocol 4-1 for trauma management.
Detective's on scene. We need to follow trauma management for a possible assault case.
We're holding for police while stabilizing and alerted the ED to the combative behavior. ED is preparing for Protocol 4-1.
No DCAP-BLS findings noted on head, chest, or extremities.
TRDP to the abdomen is negative.
No angulation in the extremities is noted at this time.
First 12-lead EKG attempt failed due to patient movement.
Second 12-lead EKG attempt also failed - motion artifact interferes. ED will need to repeat to clarify.
Third 12-lead attempt limited by movement but findings noted for the ED.
Let's secure this patient onto the stretcher. If she keeps resisting, we may have to retrain for transport.
Loading her onto the ambulance stretcher. PD is helping.
She aggressively removed the EKG leads and SpO2. Dropped the cuff. No further attempt made.
She does not have her phone or insurance. No identification available.
EMS unit is defined for VCU ED. Requesting confirmation.
She took off seatbelts and tried to throw herself sideways. VCU PD helped her to the hospital cot.
Confirmed transportation to VCU ED. Puts on secured per protocol.
One 12-lead EKG was captured with motion. Probable subendocardial injury findings noted, but for ED.
ED was advised about findings and combative behavior. One stable result recorded.
Pupils are reactive. Gross visual examination is normal.
Fourth 12-lead EKG attempt failed again. ED noted successful attempts for review.
Chest movement is symmetric. Breath sounds are present bilaterally.
Abdomen is soft with no distention, rigidity, or guarding.
Results don't reveal a murmur, but motion artifact limits clarity. ED will review.
No medications administered at this time due to patient's resistance.
Final 12-lead EKG note marked as incomplete but with findings shared for ED.
Retaking pulse - 93. No change from last reading. Oxygen still at 99%.
How is the pain now? Is it throbbing or sharp?
Still a 10. It's random. Sometimes it's like needle-like.
Fourth 12-lead attempt failed again. ED must take another reading on arrival.
Final 12-lead EKG recording notes again failed due to patient movement. ED review advised.
Briana, do you take any medications regularly?
No. I just take naproxen for the fibromyalgia sometimes.
You don't have a phone or ID with you, right?
No. I don't know my personal info, only my name and that I was assaulted.
Confirmed en route to VCU ED for complex care and combative patient.
She remains combative. PD assistance is ongoing.
EMS and police are both en route to VCU ED. Final preparations.
She has a noted possible history of fibromyalgia. Altered mental status noted.
Vital signs stable. Pt continued to be responsive but requires physical containment.
EKG interpretations were unclear but noted as subendocardial injury. Shared with ED.
Reattachment to monitoring temporarily suspended due to patient safety.
Load into unit and securing to ambulance. Anticipated arrival at VCU.
Pt remains combative during vehicle entrance. PD still present on side.
Vitals logged. Documentation finalized. Unit on trajectory towards hospital.
Possible ETOH involved, correlating to patient's behavior and confusion.
Medical history includes fibromyalgia. Noted upon arrival.
EKG logs provided include four attempts, all with motion artifact during recording.
On route to VCU ED. No additional patient data to obtain.
Attempts to stabilize ceased due to patient behavior.
Pulse 93, respirations 22. Holding steady en route.
No signs of deterioration. Pt remains combative and uncooperative.
PD monitoring patient behavior for ED handover.
What about the pain now? Is it radiating or localized?
Everything is hurting. It feels like I've been hit.
Pt unable to provide narrative. Must alert ED about for assessment.
Complete documentation provided before unloading.
Handover set for ED. PD will stay for arrival.
No patient data missing. PCRs logged and ready.
ED documentation complete. Pt combative and possible trauma.
ED prepared. EMS to be handed off at arrival.
Complete records and PCR supplied. Patient data as noted.
ED signed off upon entry. Disposition instruction provided.
Pt lacks SSN or phone number - no means for self-identify provided.
Pt fully off-EMS. All monitoring discontinued at ED area.
Equipment staged for ED and team accounted for.
Ambulance cleared. Transport details finalized.
Scene-to-hospital logs complete. All actions recorded in PCR.
Initial actions documented as DOM. ED alerted.
Handover successful. PCR finalized and sent.
Transfer complete. ED provided with PCR reports.
